chapter_title.en: Chapter on Livelihoods, Earnings, Profits, and Professions

text: 3589 - وَ رَوَى أَيُّوبُ بْنُ اَلْحُرِّ عَنْ أَبِي بَصِيرٍ عَنْ أَبِي عَبْدِ اَللَّهِ عَنْ أَبِيهِ عَلَيْهِمَا اَلسَّلاَمُ قَالَ : "لاَ بَأْسَ بِأَجْرِ اَلنَّائِحَةِ اَلَّتِي تَنُوحُ عَلَى اَلْمَيِّتِ وَ أَجْرِ اَلْمُغَنِّيَةِ اَلَّتِي تَزُفُّ اَلْعَرَائِسَ لَيْسَ بِهِ بَأْسٌ وَ لَيْسَتْ بِالَّتِي يَدْخُلُ عَلَيْهَا اَلرِّجَالُ ".

en.bab-ul-qaim-publications:
Hadith.3589 - It is narrated from Ayyub ibn Al-Hurr, from Abu Basir, from Abu Abdillah Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq (as) from his father, Imam Muhammad ibn Ali Al-Baqir (as), who said:
"There is no harm in the payment for a wailing woman who mourns the deceased, and the payment for a singer who escorts brides; there is no harm in it, as long as she is not someone whom men enter upon."
